message.content:
My fellow citizens,

In these times of rapid change and hidden threats, it is our sacred duty as leaders to protect the sovereignty and unity of our great nation. Today, I address you as your Minister of the Interior to explain a vital measure we have enacted: the prohibition of independent non-governmental organizations, or NGOs, operating without the direct oversight of our government. This is not a whim or an act of repression, but a necessary shield against forces that seek to undermine our stability and prosperity.

Consider the dangers these so-called "independent" NGOs pose. They masquerade as benevolent helpers—offering aid, promoting rights, or championing causes—but too often they serve as Trojan horses for foreign agendas. Funded by outsiders with their own interests, these groups inject alien ideologies into our society, sowing division and discord. They whisper of "democracy" and "human rights" in ways that erode our time-tested values, pitting citizen against citizen, region against region, and class against class. We have seen it before: NGOs that claim to fight poverty instead amplify grievances, turning grateful recipients into agitators; those purporting to monitor elections stir unrest by questioning our legitimate processes; and others, under the guise of environmentalism, halt our essential development projects to please distant masters.

Why are they dangerous? Because independence is an illusion. True independence means allegiance to no one but our nation—yet these NGOs answer to foreign donors, shadowy foundations, and international cabals who view our sovereignty as an obstacle to their global designs. They collect data on our people without accountability, influence our youth through unfiltered programs, and even coordinate with external media to fabricate narratives of oppression. In our intelligence reports, we have concrete evidence: funds traced from adversarial governments, training sessions in subversion disguised as workshops, and collaborations that weaken our borders and economy. Left unchecked, they fracture the national fabric, inviting chaos as we witnessed in neighboring states where "civil society" became a cover for color revolutions and economic sabotage.

Central control is not control for its own sake—it is the essence of our strength. Under our guidance, all charitable and developmental work flourishes through state-approved channels: our National Welfare Agency has lifted millions from poverty with transparent aid; our Unity Councils foster genuine dialogue without foreign meddling; and our Development Corps builds infrastructure at a pace NGOs could never match. By channeling these efforts through a single, accountable authority, we ensure resources serve the people, not hidden puppeteers. Every citizen benefits equally, every project aligns with our shared vision, and every voice is heard within the harmony of our collective will.

This ban is a declaration of self-reliance. Join us in vigilance. Report suspicious activities. Together, we preserve the peace, prosperity, and pride that define our nation. Our way is the proven path—unwavering, united, victorious.

Thank you. Long live our people!
